title: (No AI) 4 days left to save up to $190 on TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026

creator: Lauren Forristal
title: (No AI) Fika Jobs raises $4M to build a video-first hiring platform where AI agents interview candidates
dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
content: Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok.
contentSnippet: Stockholm-based startup Fika Jobs is building a video-first hiring platform that combines AI interview agents with short-form video profiles, creating something that feels like a cross between LinkedIn and TikTok.

title: (No AI) Sony’s AI Camera Assistant is exactly as bad as it looks
author: Dominic Preston
content:
![A collage of four photos of flowers, all with very different image processing from the Sony AI Camera Assistant](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/sony-xperia-1-viii-ai-camera-assistant-samples.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

I’m sorry Sony, your AI isn’t very good at photography.

When Sony announced the Xperia 1 VIII last month, it promoted the phone by sharing some of the [worst photos taken on a Sony camera in years](https://www.theverge.com/android/930459/sony-these-photos-look-terrible). These weren't just any photos, though: they were taken with Sony's new AI Camera Assistant. After a week with the Xperia 1 VIII, I'm here to tell you that the AI assistant is exactly as bad as Sony made it look.

After Sony first showed me the AI Camera Assistant during a press briefing for the Xperia 1 VIII, [I said](https://www.theverge.com/tech/927928/sony-xperia-viii-release-date-price-specs) it looked "like an improved version of Google's Camera Coach." It's pretty clear I got that wrong. Camera Coach, found on the latest Pixel phones, is a dedicated camera mode that talks you t …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/954676/sony-xperia-1-viii-ai-camera-assistant)

After a week with the Xperia 1 VIII, I'm here to tell you that the AI assistant is exactly as bad as Sony made it look.

Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without Ray-Ban
author: Victoria Song
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/MetaGlasses_1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=8.55,21.565660205136,82.25546875,73.01650212582)

For the past three years, "Meta" and "Ray-Ban" have been synonymous in the smart glasses space. Not anymore. Yesterday, I slipped on several pairs of Meta Glasses - no Ray-Bans - in three different styles and seven colors. One style, I was told several times by various enthusiastic Meta spokespeople, is a collaboration with socialite and reality TV star Kylie Jenner.

Meta could've struck out on its own from the get-go, but teaming up with EssilorLuxottica and the Ray-Ban brand was a savvy move in entering the smart glasses space. Previous smart glasses looked straight out of a sci-fi flick, or were just dorky enough to be unappealing. Ray-B …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/954052/meta-glasses-hands-on-kylie-jenner-smart-glasses-price-battery-privacy)

title: (No AI) Meta Pauses Employee-Tracking Program Following Internal Data Leak

content: The move comes after the company left potentially sensitive data from the initiative exposed internally.

title: (No AI) Meta Exposed Data Internally From Its Controversial Employee-Tracking Program

content: Employees had previously raised concerns about the initiative, which involves collecting workers’ keystroke data to train AI models.
